editorial.title: [사설] 전 세계가 긴축 전환인데 한국만 “긴축 재정은 포퓰리즘”
editorial.body: 전 세계가 긴축 전환인데 한국만 긴축 재정은 포퓰리즘 트럼프 미 대통령이 임명한 케빈 워시 연방준비제도 의장이 처음 주재한 연방공개시장위원회FOMC에서 기준금리를 연 3.5~3.75%로 동결했지만, 연내 인상 방침을 명확히 했다. 연준은 인플레이션이 여전히 2%
editorial.pullQuote: 전 세계가 긴축 전환인데 한국만 '긴축 재정은 포퓰리즘'이라 한다.
summary: 세계는 긴축으로 전환 중인데 한국만 다른 길을 가고 있다.
미 연준은 기준금리를 동결하되 연내 인상을 명확히 했다.
한국의 포퓰리즘적 재정 정책이 글로벌 트렌드와 맞지 않는다.
editorial.stance: 한국도 세계적 긴축 기조에 맞춰야 한다는 입장
editorial.titleTr: Editorial: While the World Turns to Austerity, Korea Alone Calls Fiscal Restraint 'Populism
summaryTr: The world is shifting toward austerity, but Korea is taking a different path.
The Federal Reserve held its benchmark rate steady while clearly signaling rate hikes later this year.
Korea's populist fiscal policies are out of step with global trends.
editorial.bodyTr: The whole world is shifting toward fiscal austerity, but Korea alone treats austerity as populism. At the Federal Open Market Committee chaired for the first time by Kevin Warsh, the Federal Reserve chairman appointed by U.S. President Trump, the benchmark interest rate was held at an annual 3.5 to 3.75 percent. However, the Fed made clear its intention to raise rates within the year. The Fed views inflation as still above the 2 percent target.

editorials.titleTr: [Editorial] Korea's First SMR Confirmed, Must Nurture K-Nuclear as Next Growth Engine After Semiconductors
summaryTr: Korea Hydro and Nuclear Power has finalized Gijang County in Busan as the country's first SMR construction site.
The company is launching a small modular reactor project with a target of commercial operation by 2035.
South Korea must cultivate the future of K-nuclear power through factory modular production.
editorials.bodyTr:
Korea's First SMR Confirmed, Must Nurture K-Nuclear as Future to Follow Semiconductors

Korea Hydro and Nuclear Power has finalized Gijang County in Busan as the domestic site for Korea's first small modular reactor, taking its first step toward commercial operation by 2035. SMRs place core equipment in a single vessel and mass-produce modules in factories for on-site assembly.

editorials.titleTr: Editorial: Senior Bus Fare Subsidies for Those Over 70 Should Be Coordinated With Subway Free-Ride Age Adjustment
summaryTr: The Seoul City Council has passed an ordinance to subsidize bus fares for passengers aged 70 and older.
The policy expands the existing free subway benefit to bus services.
There is an argument that it should be coordinated with adjustments to the free subway age eligibility.
editorials.bodyTr: The Seoul Metropolitan Council's standing committee has passed an ordinance to expand subsidies for seniors' public transportation fares from the existing subway system to buses. According to reports, the measure would have Seoul City subsidize fares for seniors aged 70 and older on city buses and community buses. However, this expansion of fare support must be coordinated with adjustments to the age threshold for free subway rides for the elderly.

editorial.title: [사설] 감시 무풍지대 선관위의 끝 모를 도덕적 해이
editorial.body: 입력 2026.06.18 10:33 2026.06.18 09:43 2026.06.18 06:09 노태악 당시 중앙선거관리위원장이 지난 5일 ‘투표용지 부족 사태’ 관련 대국민 사과를 하고 있다. 뉴스1 중앙선거관리위원회가 투표용지 부족 사태를 초래한 6·3 지방선거 당시 일부 투표소에서 극심한 혼란상이 나타났던 것으로 확인되고 있다. 선거 당일 상황을 기록한 투표록에 따르면, 서울 잠실2동 한 투표소에선 투표관리관 도장이 찍히지 않은 채 용지가 배부됐다. 다른 투표소에선 바닥에 투표용지가 떨어져 있거나, 용지 부족 때문에 선관위에 전화를 걸어도 연락이 닿지 않았다고 한다. 추가 투표용지가 도착한 뒤에도 대기표를 받았던 주민들이 돌아오지 않아 참정권 행사가 불발됐다. 지난해 대선 때도 다른 사람의 선거인명부에 서명하고 투표한 사례가 2000건이 넘는 등 선거 관리 전반의 부실이 심각하다. 다 이유가 있었다. 선거로 업무가 바빠지는 해에 휴직자가 오히려 늘어나는 게 이번뿐 아니라 선관위의 전통이라고 한다. 이 조직이 공정한 선거 관리를 맡을 자격이 있는지 의문을 갖게 한다. 문제는 부실에서 그치지 않는다. 선관위 지도부부터 심각한 도덕적 해이에 빠져 있었다는 사실이 속속 드러나고 있다. 노태악 전 중앙선관위원장은 지난해 덴마크·스웨덴을 비롯해 재임 중 세 차례 간 해외 출장에 모두 배우자를 동반했다. 1200만원이 넘는 비즈니스 항공권 등의 경비를 선관위가 지불했다. 공식적인 외교나 국가 중대사도 아닌 해외 선거 관련 방문에 세금을 흥청망청 쓴 것이어서 외유성 출장이라는 비판을 면키 어렵다. 외부에 공개되는 출장 보고서에 ‘부부 동반’ 문구를 넣지 않은 것만 봐도 명백한 특혜가 아닐 수 없다. 기관장이 이러니 직원들의 일탈은 예고된 것이나 마찬가지였다. 선관위 직원들은 몰디브 등 세계적인 휴양지로 출장을 가고 선거 홍보물로 활용된다며 해변 사진을 버젓이 보고서에 첨부했다. 역량 강화를 이유로 이탈리아 등 관광지에도 매년 직원을 보냈다. 심지어 선관위 고위직들의 ‘자녀 특혜 채용’ 의혹이 불거진 시기에도 외유성 출장이 계속될 정도로 내부 기강 해이가 심각하다. 독립성을 방패 삼아 외부의 감시와 견제를 피해 성역을 구축해 온 선관위는 전면적인 개혁 없이는 국민의 신뢰를 회복할 수 없는 지경에 이르렀다. 예산 집행의 투명성 확보와 함께 채용 비리를 차단할 감시 시스템이 도입돼야 한다. 신뢰를 잃은 선관위가 관리하는 선거는 결과에 대한 불신의 씨앗을 남길 우려가 있다.
editorial.pullQuote: 기관장이 이러니 직원들의 일탈은 예고된 것이나 마찬가지였다.
summary: 6·3 지방선거에서 투표용지 부족으로 극심한 혼란 발생, 선관위 관리 부실 심각
노태악 전 위원장이 배우자 동반 해외출장에 1200만원대 세금 낭비
선관위는 독립성을 방패 삼아 감시를 피해온 후 전면 개혁 없이는 신뢰 회복 불가
editorial.stance: 선관위의 독립성 뒤에 숨은 적폐를 척결하고 예산·채용 감시 시스템을 도입해 민주주의의 기초인 선거 신뢰를 회복해야 한다
editorial.titleTr: Editorial: The Election Commission's Bottomless Moral Hazard in the Surveillance Gray Zone
summaryTr: Severe chaos erupted during the June 3 local elections due to ballot shortages, revealing serious negligence in election commission management
Former Chairman Noh Tae-ak wasted over 12 million won in taxpayer money on overseas business trips accompanied by his spouse
The election commission has evaded oversight by using independence as a shield, and trust cannot be restored without comprehensive reform
editorial.bodyTr: The National Election Commission has confirmed that severe chaos occurred at some polling places during the June 3 local elections that led to the ballot shortage crisis. According to voting records documenting the situation on election day, at one polling station in Jamsil 2-dong, Seoul, ballots were distributed without the election supervisor's stamp. At other polling stations, ballots were found scattered on the floor, and residents reported that calls to the election commission went unanswered due to ballot shortages. Even after additional ballots arrived, residents who had received waiting tickets did not return, resulting in lost voting opportunities. During last year's presidential election, there were over 2,000 cases of people signing other voters' names and casting ballots, indicating serious mismanagement across the entire election administration system. There were reasons for this. According to reports, it is a tradition at the election commission that the number of employees on leave actually increases during years when election work becomes busy. This raises questions about whether this organization is qualified to manage fair elections. The problem extends beyond mere negligence. It is becoming increasingly clear that the election commission's leadership has fallen into serious ethical lapses. Former Election Commission Chairman Roh Tae-ak accompanied his spouse on all three overseas business trips during his tenure, including visits to Denmark and Sweden. The election commission paid for business-class airfare exceeding 12 million won and other expenses. Using taxpayer money for overseas visits related to elections that were neither official diplomatic missions nor major state affairs is difficult to defend against criticism of junket travel. The fact that official trip reports did not mention "accompanied by spouse" makes clear that this was outright favoritism. Given the conduct of the agency head, employee misconduct was virtually inevitable. Election commission staff went on business trips to world-renowned vacation destinations like the Maldives and brazenly attached beach photos to reports, claiming they would be used in election promotional materials. They sent employees yearly to tourist destinations like Italy under the guise of capacity building. Internal discipline has deteriorated so severely that even when suspicions arose of "nepotistic hiring" among senior election commission officials, junket travel continued unabated. The election commission, using independence as a shield to avoid external oversight and checks while creating a sanctuary, has reached a point where it cannot recover public trust without comprehensive reform. Transparency in budget execution must be secured, and a monitoring system to prevent hiring irregularities must be introduced. Elections managed by an election commission that has lost public trust risk leaving seeds of doubt about their outcomes.

leanScore.label: Lean progressive
leanScore.rationale: 공공기관의 도덕적 해이와 특권, 투명성 부족을 강하게 비판하며 감시 체계 강화를 요구하는 진보적 프레이밍으로, 권력 기관의 부실과 기강 해이에 대한 진보진영의 전형적 관점을 드러냄.

editorial.title: Seasonal stalemate
editorial.body: Korea must rethink minimum wage politics for a changing economy Every summer, South Korea performs a ritual as predictable as the monsoon. Labor and business representatives gather, exchange familiar accusations and eventually settle on a compromise that satisfies neither side. The choreography rarely changes. The economy beneath it, however, has changed profoundly. Organized labor has proposed raising next year's minimum wage by 16.3 percent to 12,000 won ($7.90) an hour. Employers are again demanding industry-specific rates, arguing that a single nationwide standard no longer reflects reality. The debate sounds familiar; the underlying economy does not. South Korea's minimum wage, now 10,320 won, already ranks among the highest in East Asia. Yet the annual dispute has become more than an argument over purchasing power and business costs. It increasingly exposes an economy split between globally competitive industries and domestic sectors struggling to remain viable. Workers have legitimate grievances. Average minimum wage increases over the past three years stood at 2.37 percent, below the average inflation rate of 2.66 percent. Real incomes for low-wage earners have therefore lost ground. Semiconductor windfalls and widening asset inequality have reinforced perceptions of a two-speed economy. Against that backdrop, an hourly wage that barely covers the cost of a simple lunch in Seoul has become a tangible measure of how unevenly prosperity is distributed. The same two-speed economy that frustrates workers also shapes employers' capacity to pay. According to employer groups, value added per worker in accommodation and food services is around 28 million won, barely one-sixth of manufacturing's 170 million won. The strain is already visible. More than 31 percent of businesses in accommodation and food services failed to meet the statutory minimum wage last year. That suggests less a disregard for the law than an inability to comply. In those industries, the minimum wage already amounts to roughly 87 percent of the median wage, leaving little room to absorb additional costs. The consequences extend beyond corporate balance sheets. Faced with mounting labor expenses, many small businesses are turning to kiosks, self-service systems and robots. Entry-level jobs often disappear first. The timing could hardly be worse. Permanent employment recently declined for the first time in more than 26 years, with much of the weakness concentrated among workers in their 20s and 30s. More than 500,000 university graduates are unemployed, a level not seen since the pandemic years. Public finances are hardly immune. Because unemployment benefits are pegged to the minimum wage, sharp increases in the statutory wage floor feed directly into welfare spending. Last year's jobless benefits reached a record 17.5 trillion won, while the employment insurance fund has fallen deep into deficit. The system itself has struggled to adapt. The Minimum Wage Act has permitted industry-specific rates since 1988, but the provision has been used only once. Labor groups fear differentiated wages would stigmatize certain occupations and worsen labor shortages. Those concerns deserve respect. Yet mechanical equality can produce distortions of its own when economic conditions vary so widely. A nation that prides itself on technological sophistication should not assume that identical rules automatically guarantee fairness. Neither freezing wages nor imposing large increases offers a durable answer. A more sensible approach would be to start pilot programs in the most vulnerable sectors, accompanied by targeted tax relief, productivity-enhancing support for microbusinesses and welfare reforms less dependent on the minimum wage itself. Every summer brings another season of familiar arguments. Rituals have their place, but they are poor substitutes for adaptation. South Korea’s challenge is no longer choosing between labor and management. It is adapting institutions to an economy of widening disparities, while recognizing that social protection cannot rest indefinitely on the payrolls of private employers. Trump signals interest in N. summary: South Korea's annual minimum wage debate masks a deeper problem: a two-speed economy where some sectors can't afford raises.
Labor demands 16.3% wage hike to 12,000 won; employers seek industry-specific rates as accommodation and food services struggle.
Rigid wage rules risk job losses through automation, while pilot programs and targeted support offer a smarter path forward.
editorial.stance: Korea needs pragmatic wage reform beyond annual ritual—pilot programs and targeted support, not mechanical equality or freezes.
summaryTr: 한국의 연간 최저임금 논쟁은 더 깊은 문제를 감추고 있다: 일부 업종이 임금 인상을 감당할 수 없는 이중 속도의 경제.
노동계는 시간당 12,000원으로 16.3% 임금 인상을 요구하고 있으며, 숙박·음식업이 어려움을 겪으면서 경영계는 업종별 차등 임금을 추진 중이다.
경직된 임금 규제는 자동화를 통한 일자리 손실 위험을 초래하는 반면, 시범 사업과 표적화된 지원이 더 현명한 길을 제시한다.
editorial.bodyTr: 한국은 변화하는 경제에 맞춰 최저임금 정치를 재고해야 한다 매년 여름 남한은 몬순만큼 예측 가능한 의식을 치러낸다. 노동계와 경영계 대표들이 모여 익숙한 비난을 주고받다가 결국 어느 쪽도 만족시키지 못하는 타협에 도달한다. 이 관례는 거의 변하지 않는다. 하지만 그 아래에 있는 경제는 깊이 있게 변했다. 조직 노동계는 내년도 최저임금을 16.3% 인상하여 시간당 1만2000원(7.90달러)으로 올릴 것을 제안했다. 고용주들은 다시금 업종별 차등 임금제를 요구하면서 하나의 전국 통일 기준이 더 이상 현실을 반영하지 않는다고 주장하고 있다. 논쟁은 익숙하게 들리지만 그 아래의 경제는 그렇지 않다. 현재 1만320원인 한국의 최저임금은 이미 동아시아에서 가장 높은 수준에 속한다. 그러나 매년 반복되는 이 분쟁은 단순한 구매력과 경영비용 논쟁을 넘어섰다. 이는 점차 세계 경쟁력을 갖춘 산업과 생존을 위해 고군분투하는 국내 부문 사이의 이중 경제를 드러내고 있다. 노동자들의 불만은 정당하다. 지난 3년간 최저임금 평균 인상률은 2.37%로 평균 물가상승률 2.66%보다 낮았다. 따라서 저임금 근로자의 실질 소득은 감소했다. 반도체 호황과 자산 불평등 심화는 이중 속도 경제에 대한 인식을 강화했다. 이러한 배경에서 서울에서 간단한 점심 한 끼 비용도 겨우 충당하는 시간당 임금은 번영이 얼마나 불균등하게 분배되고 있는지를 보여주는 구체적인 지표가 되었다. 노동자들을 답답하게 하는 바로 그 이중 속도 경제가 고용주들의 임금 지급 능력을 좌우한다. 경영계에 따르면 숙박 및 음식 서비스 산업의 근로자 1인당 부가가치는 약 2800만원으로 제조업의 1억7000만원의 6분의 1에 불과하다. 그 부담은 이미 가시화되고 있다. 지난해 숙박 및 음식 서비스 산업 기업의 31% 이상이 법정 최저임금을 충족하지 못했다. 이는 법을 무시한다기보다 준수할 능력이 없음을 시사한다. 이들 산업에서 최저임금은 이미 중위임금의 약 87%에 해당하며, 추가 비용을 흡수할 여지가 거의 없다. 그 영향은 기업 재무제표를 넘어선다. 급증하는 노동 비용에 직면한 많은 소규모 기업들은 키오스크, 셀프 서비스 시스템, 로봇으로 눈을 돌리고 있다. 초급 일자리가 가장 먼저 사라진다. 이 시점이 더 좋을 수 없다. 상용직 고용이 26년 이상 만에 처음으로 감소했으며, 약세는 20~30대 근로자에게 집중되어 있다. 500만명 이상의 대졸 미취업자가 있는데, 이는 팬데믹 시기 이후 보지 못한 수준이다. 공공 재정도 면역이 아니다. 실업급여가 최저임금에 연동되어 있기 때문에 법정 임금 하한선의 급격한 인상은 직접 복지 지출로 이어진다. 지난해 실업급여는 17조5000억원으로 사상 최고치를 기록했으며, 고용보험 기금은 심각한 적자 상태에 빠져 있다. 제도 자체도 적응하기 위해 고군분투했다. 최저임금법은 1988년 이후 업종별 차등 임금제를 허용했지만, 그 조항은 단 한 번만 사용됐다. 노동 단체들은 차등 임금이 특정 직업에 낙인을 찍고 노동력 부족을 악화시킬 것을 우려한다. 그러한 우려는 존중할 만하다. 그러나 경제 상황이 매우 다를 때 기계적 평등은 그 자체의 왜곡을 초래할 수 있다. 기술 정교함을 자랑하는 국가가 동일한 규칙이 자동으로 공정성을 보장한다고 가정해서는 안 된다. 임금 동결이든 대규모 인상이든 지속 가능한 답을 제시하지 못한다. 더 현명한 접근 방식은 가장 취약한 부문에서 시범 프로그램을 시작하되, 표적화된 세제 감면, 소규모 자영업자의 생산성 향상 지원, 최저임금 자체에 덜 의존하는 복지 개혁을 함께 추진하는 것이다. 매년 여름 또 다른 익숙한 주장의 계절이 온다. 의식은 그 자리가 있지만, 적응의 대체재로는 부족하다. 한국의 과제는 더 이상 노동계와 경영계 중 선택하는 것이 아니다. 불평등이 심화되는 경제에 제도를 적응시키면서도 사회 보호가 민간 고용주의 급여에 무한정 의존할 수 없다는 점을 인식하는 것이다.

leanScore.label: Center
leanScore.rationale: The editorial acknowledges legitimate worker grievances (real wage loss, inequality) and employer constraints (business failures, automation pressures) with equal weight, then proposes pragmatic compromise (pilot programs, tax relief, welfare reform) rather than advancing either labor or management interests.

editorials.title: Seasonal stalemate
editorials.body: Korea must rethink minimum wage politics for a changing economy Every summer, South Korea performs a ritual as predictable as the monsoon. Labor and business representatives gather, exchange familiar accusations and eventually settle on a compromise that satisfies neither side. The choreography rarely changes. The economy beneath it, however, has changed profoundly. Organized labor has proposed raising next year's minimum wage by 16.3 percent to 12,000 won ($7.90) an hour. Employers are again demanding industry-specific rates, arguing that a single nationwide standard no longer reflects reality. The debate sounds familiar; the underlying economy does not. South Korea's minimum wage, now 10,320 won, already ranks among the highest in East Asia. Yet the annual dispute has become more than an argument over purchasing power and business costs. It increasingly exposes an economy split between globally competitive industries and domestic sectors struggling to remain viable. Workers have legitimate grievances. Average minimum wage increases over the past three years stood at 2.37 percent, below the average inflation rate of 2.66 percent. Real incomes for low-wage earners have therefore lost ground. Semiconductor windfalls and widening asset inequality have reinforced perceptions of a two-speed economy. Against that backdrop, an hourly wage that barely covers the cost of a simple lunch in Seoul has become a tangible measure of how unevenly prosperity is distributed. The same two-speed economy that frustrates workers also shapes employers' capacity to pay. According to employer groups, value added per worker in accommodation and food services is around 28 million won, barely one-sixth of manufacturing's 170 million won. The strain is already visible. More than 31 percent of businesses in accommodation and food services failed to meet the statutory minimum wage last year. That suggests less a disregard for the law than an inability to comply. In those industries, the minimum wage already amounts to roughly 87 percent of the median wage, leaving little room to absorb additional costs. The consequences extend beyond corporate balance sheets. Faced with mounting labor expenses, many small businesses are turning to kiosks, self-service systems and robots. Entry-level jobs often disappear first. The timing could hardly be worse. Permanent employment recently declined for the first time in more than 26 years, with much of the weakness concentrated among workers in their 20s and 30s. More than 500,000 university graduates are unemployed, a level not seen since the pandemic years. Public finances are hardly immune. Because unemployment benefits are pegged to the minimum wage, sharp increases in the statutory wage floor feed directly into welfare spending. Last year's jobless benefits reached a record 17.5 trillion won, while the employment insurance fund has fallen deep into deficit. The system itself has struggled to adapt. The Minimum Wage Act has permitted industry-specific rates since 1988, but the provision has been used only once. Labor groups fear differentiated wages would stigmatize certain occupations and worsen labor shortages. Those concerns deserve respect. Yet mechanical equality can produce distortions of its own when economic conditions vary so widely. A nation that prides itself on technological sophistication should not assume that identical rules automatically guarantee fairness. Neither freezing wages nor imposing large increases offers a durable answer. A more sensible approach would be to start pilot programs in the most vulnerable sectors, accompanied by targeted tax relief, productivity-enhancing support for microbusinesses and welfare reforms less dependent on the minimum wage itself. Every summer brings another season of familiar arguments. Rituals have their place, but they are poor substitutes for adaptation. South Korea’s challenge is no longer choosing between labor and management. It is adapting institutions to an economy of widening disparities, while recognizing that social protection cannot rest indefinitely on the payrolls of private employers. Trump signals interest in N. Korea, but Tehran playbook may not apply Speculation has intensified following Trump's social media post last week featuring a photograph from his 2018 summit with North Korean leader Kim Jong-un and his exchange with President Lee Jae Myung at the G7 summit in France this week. summary: South Korea's annual minimum wage debate masks a deeper problem: a two-speed economy where some sectors can't afford raises.
Labor demands 16.3% wage hike to 12,000 won; employers seek industry-specific rates as accommodation and food services struggle.
Rigid wage rules risk job losses through automation, while pilot programs and targeted support offer a smarter path forward.
editorials.stance: Korea needs pragmatic wage reform beyond annual ritual—pilot programs and targeted support, not mechanical equality or freezes.

editorial.title: [사설] 주가·부동산 동시 상승, 금리 상승기에 대비해야
editorial.body: 코스피가 18일 사상 처음 9000을 돌파했다. 미국-이란 전쟁 종전 소식에 반도체주 중심으로 증시에 돈이 쏠리고, 부동산값 상승 기대도 높아져 일부 서울·경기 아파트에 매수세가 몰리는 양상이다. 이날 코스피는 전날보다 2.25% 오른 9063.84에 마감했다. 국내
summary: 코스피가 사상 처음 9000을 돌파하며 역사적 고점을 기록했다.

editorial.stance: 미·이란 종전은 중동의 기존 국제질서를 재정의하는 게임 체인저로 작용할 수 있으며 신중한 외교적 대응이 필요하다.
editorial.titleTr: [Editorial] U.S.-Iran Peace Deal Signing...Will It Upend the Middle East Order?
summaryTr: The United States and Iran have signed an armistice agreement, signaling a shift in the restructuring of Middle Eastern order.
The position of existing allies such as Israel and Saudi Arabia could be shaken.
There is a significant possibility that this will become a turning point fundamentally reshaping the regional power structure in the Middle East.
